title: Granulate Formulation Protocol
author: Andreea S
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bkrpkv5n

steps.title: Grow a single colony of bacteria...
steps.instruction: Grow a single colony of bacteria in an assay tube with 5 mL of Luria Broth (LB) medium

steps.title: Incubate it in an orbital shaker at 200 rev
steps.instruction: Incubate it in an orbital shaker at 200 rev.min -1 at 30 °C for 18:00:00 .

steps.title: After bacterial growth, take a 500...
steps.instruction: After bacterial growth, take a 500 mL Erlenmeyer flask and add 100 mL of LB medium in it.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of the culture...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of the culture ( 1 % (v/v) ) in the flask and incubate at 30 °C and 200 rev.min -1 for 24:00:00 , until an optical density of close to 1 is obtained.

steps.title: Add 100 mL of Difco Sporulation...
steps.instruction: Add 100 mL of Difco Sporulation Medium (DSM; 5gl -1 peptone, 3gl -1 yeast extract, 1gl -1 KCl and 0.12 gl -1 MgSO47H2O) in a 500 mL Erlenmeyer flask.

steps.title: Sterilize this DSM medium at 121 °C and 1
steps.instruction: Sterilize this DSM medium at 121 °C and 1.5 psi for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Pack it in plastic bags and...
steps.instruction: Pack it in plastic bags and store it at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 1 mL each of
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL each of: 1 Molarity (M) Ca(NO 3 ) 2 , 10 millimolar (mM) MnCl 2­ .4H 2 O and 1 millimolar (mM) FeSO4

steps.title: Mix talc (which will be the...
steps.instruction: Mix talc (which will be the carrier) with, carboxy-methyl-cellulose (CMC; 1% w/w), CaCO 3 (15% w/w) and glucose (0.25% w/w) in powder form.

steps.title: Inoculate with 1X10 6 c
steps.instruction: Inoculate with 1X10 6 c.f.u ml -1 of the bacterial strain. Keep the culture conditions at 30 °C and 200 rev.min -1 for 72:00:00 .

steps.title: Mix the same material with the...
steps.instruction: Mix the same material with the bacterial spore suspension and dry at 55 °C for 36:00:00 .

steps.title: Autoclave the mixture at 121 °C...
steps.instruction: Autoclave the mixture at 121 °C and 15 psi for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Using sterile porcelain mortar and pestle pulverize the formulation
steps.instruction: Using sterile porcelain mortar and pestle pulverize the formulation.

steps.title: Colony Forming Units (CFU) can be...
steps.instruction: Colony Forming Units (CFU) can be determined by estimating the OD of spore suspension using a tube-reading spectrophotometer adjusted at 1.978 [corresponding to 8.5 · 1010 CFU/ml] at 600nm absorbance wavelength

steps.title: The formulation will be placed on...
steps.instruction: The formulation will be placed on sterile aluminum foil in pans and air-dried for 24:00:00 with occasional stirring in a laminar airflow cabinet.

steps.title: Dried formulations (35% moisture content) of B
steps.instruction: Dried formulations (35% moisture content) of B. mycoides will be passed through a 250μm mesh sieve to attain the desired particle size.

steps.title: Pack in sterilized polypropylene bags, seal...
steps.instruction: Pack in sterilized polypropylene bags, seal and store at Room temperature prior to use.

steps.title: Count CFUs to estimate the number...
steps.instruction: Count CFUs to estimate the number of viable propagules of B. mycoides using the standard dilution platin method.

steps.title: Take three 1 g aliquots of...
steps.instruction: Take three 1 g aliquots of the dried powder and place in 99 mL sterile PBST solution (this will include PBS + 0.05 % (v/v) Tween 20). Stir magnetically at high speed for 00:15:00 . Now dilute this suspension with approximately and take 0.2 mL of this suspension and plate on Nutrient Agar (NA) media.

steps.title: By performing saponification, methylation, and then...
steps.instruction: By performing saponification, methylation, and then extracting we can obtain fatty acid methylesters from wet biomass.

steps.title: Next, separate the fatty acid methylester...
steps.instruction: Next, separate the fatty acid methylester mixtures by using a microbial identification system. Peaks can be automatically integrated, and the Microbial ID will calculate the fatty acid names and percentages.
